source.notes: Tasks translate the paper into enterprise governance assessment artifacts for human influence telemetry.

tasks.name: Human influence retention assessment
tasks.objective: Assess whether an AI-enabled workflow preserves, reduces, or transfers human influence over a decision.

required.name: workflow_description
required.description: Description of the AI-enabled workflow under review.
required.example: AI assistant produces denial-risk summaries for claims reviewers.

required.name: decision_inventory
required.description: Decisions, decision owners, AI touchpoints, review paths, and escalation paths.

optional.name: runtime_logs
optional.description: Logs showing AI recommendations, human overrides, escalations, autonomous actions, or review delays.

optional.name: baseline_process
optional.description: Description of the pre-AI workflow and human dependency.
optional.example: Human analyst reviewed every case before recommendation.

assumptions: The source is theoretical and supports governance instrumentation rather than deployment proof.
Enterprise application requires local workflow evidence.

triggers: No evidence of how AI changed human review or decision rights

acceptance_thresholds: Each finding cites at least 3 artifacts.
No high-confidence finding without named decision owner and review evidence.
evaluation.test_set_plan: Use a stratified set of enterprise AI workflows, with human reviewer labels for decision authority, feedback mechanism integrity, and evidence completeness.

governance.human_checkpoint: AI governance reviewer confirms output before it is used in advisory reporting or customer recommendations.
logging_requirements: Store human reviewer decision and timestamp

tasks.name: Local success versus systemic drift check
tasks.objective: Identify cases where AI improves local task performance while weakening human feedback, appeal, override, or expertise mechanisms.

required.name: workflow_description
required.description: Description of the AI-enabled workflow under review.
required.example: AI assistant produces denial-risk summaries for claims reviewers.

required.name: decision_inventory
required.description: Decisions, decision owners, AI touchpoints, review paths, and escalation paths.

optional.name: runtime_logs
optional.description: Logs showing AI recommendations, human overrides, escalations, autonomous actions, or review delays.

optional.name: baseline_process
optional.description: Description of the pre-AI workflow and human dependency.
optional.example: Human analyst reviewed every case before recommendation.

assumptions: The source is theoretical and supports governance instrumentation rather than deployment proof.
Enterprise application requires local workflow evidence.

triggers: No evidence of how AI changed human review or decision rights

acceptance_thresholds: Output distinguishes local KPI improvement from governance effect.
Reviewer can identify affected feedback mechanism.
evaluation.test_set_plan: Use a stratified set of enterprise AI workflows, with human reviewer labels for decision authority, feedback mechanism integrity, and evidence completeness.

governance.human_checkpoint: AI governance reviewer confirms output before it is used in advisory reporting or customer recommendations.
logging_requirements: Store human reviewer decision and timestamp

tasks.name: Cross-system influence transfer map
tasks.objective: Map how AI adoption in one workflow shifts burden or authority into another team, system, market, or governance function.

required.name: workflow_description
required.description: Description of the AI-enabled workflow under review.
required.example: AI assistant produces denial-risk summaries for claims reviewers.

required.name: decision_inventory
required.description: Decisions, decision owners, AI touchpoints, review paths, and escalation paths.

optional.name: runtime_logs
optional.description: Logs showing AI recommendations, human overrides, escalations, autonomous actions, or review delays.

optional.name: baseline_process
optional.description: Description of the pre-AI workflow and human dependency.
optional.example: Human analyst reviewed every case before recommendation.

assumptions: The source is theoretical and supports governance instrumentation rather than deployment proof.
Enterprise application requires local workflow evidence.

triggers: No evidence of how AI changed human review or decision rights

acceptance_thresholds: At least one upstream and one downstream dependency documented.
No asserted transfer without evidence of workflow dependency.
evaluation.test_set_plan: Use a stratified set of enterprise AI workflows, with human reviewer labels for decision authority, feedback mechanism integrity, and evidence completeness.

governance.human_checkpoint: AI governance reviewer confirms output before it is used in advisory reporting or customer recommendations.
logging_requirements: Store human reviewer decision and timestamp

tasks.name: Augmentation versus displacement classification
tasks.objective: Classify AI-enabled workflow changes as augmentation, partial displacement, decision-authority transfer, or feedback-loop weakening.

tasks.name: Translation boundary and reviewer gate
tasks.objective: Determine whether a gradual-disempowerment finding should remain research mapping, become evaluation design, or support a governed advisory recommendation.
